key_insight: Mn substitution at the Ni site in Cu2NiXY4 kesterites preserves the tetragonal structure while systematically narrowing the bandgap through orbital hybridization near the band edges.
engineering_relevance: This provides a simple compositional route to tune kesterite semiconductor bandgaps for optoelectronic devices such as absorbers, photodetectors, and related tunable electronic materials.
method: DFT calculations on the Cu2NiXY4 family (X = Sn, Ge, Si; Y = S, Se).
Used the mBJ+U approach to improve bandgap accuracy.
Modeled 50% Ni→Mn substitution within the kesterite unit cell due to crystallographic constraints.
evidence_signal: Across all studied compounds, the bandgap decreased from 1.028–3.397 eV to 1.007–3.333 eV after Mn substitution; Cu2NiSnS4 specifically dropped from 1.59 eV to 1.49 eV.
watch_item: The study uses a 50% substitution in the primitive kesterite cell, so lower Mn concentrations will need supercell calculations and may yield different trends.
paper_title: First-principles Study of Structural and Electronic Properties of Mn-doped Cu2NiXY4 (X=Sn, Ge, Si; Y=S, Se) Chalcogenide Semiconductors
